name: Mixed Greens and Haricots Verts with Walnut Oil Vinaigrette
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup walnut oil, or extra-virgin olive oil
1/2 pound baby lettuce, washed and spun dry
1/2 pound frisee (French or Italian), washed and spun dry
recipeInstructions:
Available at specialty foods shops or produce markets, and some supermarkets.
1. In a small jar with a tight-fitting lid, combine vinegar, oil, and salt and pepper to taste. Shake the sealed jar until the vinaigrette is emulsified. The vinaigrette may be made up to 1 day ahead and chilled. Bring the vinaigrette to room temperature before using.
2. In a saucepan of boiling salted water, cook haricots verts for 3-4 minutes or until crisp-tender. Transfer beans to a bowl of ice and cold water to stop cooking. Drain beans in a colander and pat dry between paper towels. The beans may be made up to 1 day ahead, wrapped in a paper towel in a sealable plastic bag.
3. Divide greens among 6 salad plates and top with beans. Sprinkle salads with onion and drizzle with vinaigrette.
